page: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU is equipped with 48 KB of L1D cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core performance is average, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 1897.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1660.0. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3765.27 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2998.0. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, with uncached large-block read speeds at 27.83 GB/sec and write speeds at 19.41 GB/sec. Cryptographic and database operations show similar limitations, with a Passmark database score of 2022.44 and Redis SET operations reaching 4.35 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC2s_v3 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that require a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without demanding high parallel processing power. Because it lacks local storage, users must rely on external premium storage options. The instance is appropriate for lightweight memory-intensive applications, basic text processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and small-scale LLM inference, where its average prompt processing speed of 420.8 tokens per second on a 135M model can be leveraged.
description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated x86_64 Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz with 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of RAM. The hardware lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1660.0, but weak multi-core scaling, as seen in its Geekbench multi-core score of 2998.0. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block write speeds of 19.41 GB/sec. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intensive parallel computation. It is appropriate for lightweight database operations, single-threaded file compression, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering average single-core and weak multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Average single-core performance paired with weak multi-core scaling
No local storage, requiring external premium storage solutions
Appropriate for lightweight database tasks, text processing, and small LLM inference
